abstract: The net amount, in milli-units, that you charged the customer, pre-tax, and after deducting all refunds.
overview:
## Discussion

Include this property in the line item to state the net amount in milli-units that you charged the customer, pre-tax, after you account for all refunds and restatements.

Provide all amount field values in milli-units of the currency you state in the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/reportingCurrency] field. One unit of the currency equals 1000 milli-units. For example, if the amount is `€2.99`, the amount in milli-units is `2990`.

To calculate the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/netAmountTaxExclusive], subtract the refund amount excluding taxes from the original [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/amountTaxExclusive] value or from the amount you provided in a restatement, if any.

For example: A customer makes an initial purchase for the pre-tax amount [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/amountTaxExclusive] of 1000. They receive a refund of 300. The [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/netAmountTaxExclusive] is 700 (which is the original price, 1000, minus the refund of 300).

If you then submit a restatement of the original purchase price to 800, you calculate the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/netAmountTaxExclusive] to be 500, which is the restated price, 800, minus the refund of 300.

This value is used for data validation.
platforms: Unsupported OS: External Purchase Server API

# netAmountTaxExclusive

**Type**

The net amount, in milli-units, that you charged the customer, pre-tax, and after deducting all refunds.

## Declaration

```swift
int64 netAmountTaxExclusive
```

## Discussion

Include this property in the line item to state the net amount in milli-units that you charged the customer, pre-tax, after you account for all refunds and restatements.

Provide all amount field values in milli-units of the currency you state in the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/reportingCurrency] field. One unit of the currency equals 1000 milli-units. For example, if the amount is `€2.99`, the amount in milli-units is `2990`.

To calculate the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/netAmountTaxExclusive], subtract the refund amount excluding taxes from the original [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/amountTaxExclusive] value or from the amount you provided in a restatement, if any.

For example: A customer makes an initial purchase for the pre-tax amount [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/amountTaxExclusive] of 1000. They receive a refund of 300. The [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/netAmountTaxExclusive] is 700 (which is the original price, 1000, minus the refund of 300).

If you then submit a restatement of the original purchase price to 800, you calculate the [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/netAmountTaxExclusive] to be 500, which is the restated price, 800, minus the refund of 300.

This value is used for data validation.

## Specifying amounts and currency

- **amountTaxExclusive**: The amount, in milli-units, that the customer paid or was refunded, excluding taxes.
- **amountTaxInclusive**: The amount, in milli-units, that the customer paid, including taxes.
- **taxAmount**: The amount, in milli-units, that the customer paid in taxes.
- **taxCountry**: The three-letter country code of the country that collects the taxes for the transaction.
- **pricingCurrency**: The currency used in the transaction to bill or refund the customer.
- **reportingCurrency**: The currency the line item uses to report all amount values.
- **exchangeRate**: A decimal value that is the exchange rate you use to convert the pricing currency to the reporting currency, when the two currencies differ.
